verses.1: A Psalm of David. The LORD said to my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thy enemies thy footstool.
verses.2: The LORD will send the rod of thy strength out of Zion: rule thou in the midst of thy enemies.
verses.3: Thy people shall be willing in the day of thy power, in the beauties of holiness: from the womb of the morning, thou hast the dew of thy youth.
verses.4: The LORD hath sworn, and will not repent, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedek.
verses.5: The LORD at thy right hand will strike through kings in the day of his wrath.
verses.6: He will judge among the heathen, he will fill the places with the dead bodies; he will wound the heads over many countries.
verses.7: He will drink of the brook in the way: therefore will he lift up the head.

metadata.source: Webster's Bible (1833) — presented as-is with minor editorial corrections for the ABIDE Bible App.
